title: quercus setulosa
intro: Quercus setulosa, also known as the scrub oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The scrub oak has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties due to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Quercus setulosa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus setulosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
